commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes OpenClaw-owned automatic/context-engine Codex compaction paths, starts explicit Codex compaction asynchronously via `thread/compact/start`, blocks `/compact` gateway fallback, and updates related tests, docs, and changelog.

PR surface: Source -556, Tests -722, Docs +5. Total -1273 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction for the PR defect: PR head returns pending Codex native starts as `ok && compacted`, and the unchanged gateway session compact path treats that shape as completed and mutates session state. I did not run the app, but the call contracts are direct and covered by the cited lines.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can update pending Codex compaction consumers and tests; maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed compatibility/availability change still remains after that repair.

target.title: Fail Codex compaction at the Codex boundary
target.body:
## Summary

Codex runtime compaction was still being treated like an OpenClaw-owned PI safeguard in a few places. OpenClaw could preflight-compact Codex reply turns, post-turn compact Codex CLI transcripts, let a context-engine plugin replace Codex app-server compaction, and force plugin compaction before or after Codex `turn/start` overflow handling.

That is the wrong boundary. Codex already owns automatic compaction inside the Codex runtime. OpenClaw should not predict when a Codex thread needs compaction and then rewrite the transcript around it. At best that duplicates the native harness. At worst it crosses runtime/auth boundaries, restarts the shared app-server, or drifts a Codex-backed OpenAI session into OpenClaw/public Responses summarization.

This tightens the contract. Automatic compaction for Codex runtime sessions is native Codex-owned. OpenClaw now skips its preflight and CLI automatic compaction paths for Codex, removes the Codex app-server context-engine forced-compaction paths, and no longer lets an `ownsCompaction` context engine take over Codex app-server compaction. Explicit compaction is still supported: `/compact` and plugin-requested/manual compaction requests are forwarded to Codex as native `thread/compact/start`, then OpenClaw returns immediately instead of waiting, timing out, restarting the shared app-server, or falling back to another summarizer.

The fail-closed behavior stays where it belongs. If native Codex compaction cannot be started because the native thread binding is missing or stale, OpenClaw reports that failure honestly. It does not restart the shared Codex app-server as a retry strategy, and it does not fall back to OpenClaw/public OpenAI compaction for Codex runtime sessions. Non-Codex pinned harnesses still keep their previous context-engine fallback path. The Codex harness docs now state that Codex owns native compaction and that context engines do not replace it.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-runtime sessions should not be automatically compacted by OpenClaw preflight, CLI, context-engine, or public OpenAI summarization paths. Explicit/manual/plugin compaction should only start native Codex compaction through `thread/compact/start`, without OpenClaw waiting on completion, imposing its own timeout, restarting the shared app-server, or falling back to another compaction backend.

Real environment tested: Pash's local dev agent was restarted from this branch at head `a26f2b9c5fb359cefc5b97e8a80f077059747a7b`. Gateway reported `2026.5.24` running from `/Users/pash/code/openclaw/dist/index.js`, pid `48653`, healthy loopback on `127.0.0.1:18789`. The live proof used the real `main` agent with Codex OAuth auth redacted, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, and the embedded Codex app-server harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw gateway restart`; `pnpm openclaw gateway status --deep`; `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-final-1779663763 --message "Final live proof turn 1 for PR 85958 on commit 81328167c4. Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_TURN_1_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-final-1779663763 --message "Final live proof turn 2 for PR 85958 on the same session. Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_TURN_2_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; after the docs-only head commit and a second gateway restart, `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-final-1779663763 --message "Post-docs-head live proof turn 3 for PR 85958 on branch head a26f2b9c5f after gateway restart. Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_TURN_3_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; `rg -n 'preflight_compacting|compaction-diag|falling back to context engine|Summarization failed|provider_error_4xx|"api":"openai-responses"|"modelApi":"openai-responses"|thread/compact/start|contextCompaction|gateway_timeout|EMBEDDED FALLBACK' /Users/pash/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/7b1d9666-d817-4182-b3a4-43ca985d530f*`; `rg -n 'context_compacted|contextCompaction|thread/compact|Summarization failed|api.responses.write|openai-responses|Incorrect API key|falling back to context engine' /Users/pash/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/sessions/2026/05/24/rollout-2026-05-24T16-02-55-019e5c3a-2533-7321-845e-44a55ab1545b.jsonl`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts docs/plugins/codex-harness.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md`; `pnpm lint:docs`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix: The live Gateway turns all completed in the same session, `7b1d9666-d817-4182-b3a4-43ca985d530f`, bound to Codex thread `019e5c3a-2533-7321-845e-44a55ab1545b`. Turn 1 returned exactly `FINAL_PROOF_TURN_1_OK` with run id `4a70de03-f779-488b-99d4-b2e4e362ea5b`, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, and runner `embedded`. Turn 2 returned exactly `FINAL_PROOF_TURN_2_OK` with run id `dfbb3821-57c6-44e7-972e-47e66a71f3ee`, the same session id, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, and runner `embedded`. Turn 3 after restarting the gateway on head `a26f2b9c5f` returned exactly `FINAL_PROOF_TURN_3_OK` with run id `675fc654-baed-4e20-926e-cfa576992678`, the same session id, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, and runner `embedded`. The session-file grep found no OpenClaw preflight compaction, context-engine fallback, public `openai-responses` metadata, manual compact start, Gateway timeout fallback, or embedded fallback artifacts. The Codex rollout grep found no Codex compaction failure, public OpenAI Responses summarization, `api.responses.write` failure, or context-engine fallback strings.

Observed result after fix: The live dev agent stayed on the Codex runtime for normal turns before and after a Gateway restart, resumed the same Codex-backed session, and did not drift into OpenClaw/public Responses compaction. The code-level tests now lock the policy down: Codex app-server explicit/manual compaction starts `thread/compact/start` and does not wait for notification completion, add OpenClaw timeouts, restart the app-server, call context-engine compaction, or run context-engine maintenance as if compaction had completed; `/compact` reports `Codex compaction started` for this pending native path; and a Gateway timeout on `/compact` does not create a fresh embedded fallback session. Targeted tests, changed checks, docs lint, formatting, diff whitespace checks, build, and exact-head PR CI passed locally or in GitHub Actions as applicable.

What was not tested: I did not force a destructive live compaction timeout on Pash's running dev agent session, and I did not trigger a live Discord or Telegram human-authored `/compact` command from this shell. The native compact-start path and no-fallback behavior are covered by focused tests around the same runtime seam.
